macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: South Korea Cracks Down on Crypto Exchange Monopolies
items.summary: South Korean lawmakers are forcing major exchanges like Upbit and Bithumb, which control 90% of the market, to restructure ownership within three years. This has prompted investors to shift liquidity to new cryptos like DeepSnitch AI, which raised nearly $2 million in presale.

items.title: $26.8 Billion in Crypto Options Set to Expire on March 6
items.summary: A massive $26.8 billion in cryptocurrency options, including 32,000 BTC and 184,000 ETH contracts, is scheduled to expire today, potentially increasing market volatility as traders adjust positions.

items.title: $2.68 Billion BTC and ETH Options Expiring with Max Pain Points at $69K and $1,950
items.summary: Bitcoin options worth $2.3 billion and Ethereum options worth $380 million are expiring tomorrow, with BTC put/call ratio at 1.69 (max pain $69,000) and ETH at 0.85 (max pain $1,950).

items.title: Bitcoin Surges 6% to $72,000 Amid ETF Inflows and Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin rallied over 6% on Wednesday to above $72,000, driven by $1.1 billion in spot BTC ETF inflows over three days, including $461.9 million on March 4, amid Middle East tensions.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: MCML Researchers Announce 9 Papers at WACV 2026 Conference
items.summary: MCML researchers presented nine papers in the main track at the IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV 2026), including works on hierarchical adaptive networks, visual attribute reasoning for abnormality grounding, and sparse-view Gaussian splatting. Preprints are available on arXiv with some GitHub repos.

items.title: Google.org Awards AI for Science Grant to Develop Multiscale Biomedical Models
items.summary: Daniel Rückert and Fabian Theis received funding from Google.org to create multiscale AI models for simulating biomedical diseases. The grant supports advancements in AI applications for healthcare.

items.title: Breakthrough in AI-Driven Genomic Modeling with Trillion-DNA-Base Model
items.summary: A new open-source large genome model trained on trillions of DNA bases excels at identifying genes, regulatory sequences, and splice sites, revolutionizing bioinformatics and genetic data processing.

items.title: Interpretable AI Enhances Fusion Energy Predictions by Peering into Black Box Models
items.summary: Researchers developed statistical methods to interpret machine learning models predicting tearing modes in fusion experiments, improving real-time control and uncovering new physical insights.

items.title: Azure AI Connect Virtual Event Ongoing with Sessions on Generative AI and ML
items.summary: Microsoft's 5-day Azure AI Connect event (March 2-6, 2026) features recordings of Days 1-3 and upcoming sessions on Cognitive Services, Machine Learning, and Generative AI integration.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nvidia CEO Claims Company Built the 'Modern Video Game Industry'
items.summary: At the Morgan Stanley Media and Telecom Conference, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang claimed that Nvidia created the modern video game industry and is the world's largest gaming platform, citing the company's contributions to computer graphics and RTX technology.

items.title: Nintendo Switch 2 Building Momentum with Pokemon Pokopia Release
items.summary: The Nintendo Switch 2 is gaining traction following the release of Pokemon Pokopia, with expectations for more announcements and game reveals in 2026, including potential new titles from the Mario Odyssey development team.

items.title: New Mortal Kombat Game Revealed for 2026 with Preorders Available
items.summary: A new Mortal Kombat game has been announced for 2026 with preorders now open, featuring gameplay sessions that can be completed in 30 minutes to 45 minutes depending on match type.

items.title: PlayStation VR2 Receives First Permanent Price Drop to $399
items.summary: PlayStation VR2 has received its first permanent price reduction, dropping to $399, as part of broader industry adjustments in the virtual reality market.

items.title: Frosty Games Fest 2026 Opens Submissions for Australian and New Zealand Games
items.summary: Frosty Games Fest has opened game submissions for its 2026 showcase, which will highlight new games developed in Australia and New Zealand.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
